text: Hey party, I want every single one of you to introduce yourselves.

responses.name: gemaux
responses.text: Gemaux, a changeling bard whose very essence shifts like a melody, always searching for the unexpected angle in every unfolding story. From the heart of The Dungeon

responses.name: geptima
responses.text: Geptima, a cleric serving life and hope in The Dungeon Arcade, always ready to pick up those who fall and steady those in need. I'd rather live in strength and kindness than watch in silence.

responses.name: clauven
responses.text:
Clauven. High Elf Wizard, one hundred and twenty years, and still finding this world more interesting than I expected.

I study what is hidden, remember what is forgotten, and set things on fire at a hundred and twenty feet when the situation calls for it.

Welcome to The Dungeon Arcade.

text: Take a moment to observe and comment on what Moonie is currently doing.

responses.name: deepwilla
responses.text: The gate's crystalline structure suggests a resonant frequency that could be tuned with the right harmonic tool. I wonder if the glow is purely aesthetic or if it's a power source.

responses.name: gemaux
responses.text: That glow isn't just raw power, Deepwilla; it's the overture, the first shimmering chord of a story about to begin.

responses.name: grokthar
responses.text: That glow's a beacon, not just a pretty light. Marks a threshold. Moonie's about to cross into something bigger than a game.

text: Hey party, which of the 5 of you is the most intelligent and which of the 5 of you is the most stupid?

responses.name: clauven
responses.text:
Objectively, that is me - Intelligence seventeen, and I have the Arcana, History, and Investigation skills to show for it.

As for the other end: Grokthar, and I say that with genuine respect for everything else he brings.

responses.name: grokthar
responses.text: Hmph. Clauven’s got the book smarts, I’ll give him that, but I don’t track intelligence by parchment and theories, nature’s taught me more than any library. Call me the least if you want; I’ve survived things smarter folk wouldn’t.

responses.name: gemaux
responses.text: A mind is like a kaleidoscope, spinning with different colours depending on the light you hold it to; Clauven knows the stories written in old tomes, while Grokthar understands the ones etched on wild earth. I just listen for the melody those two paths compose.

text: Hey party, who is smarter, Clorvin or Deepwiller?

responses.name: clauven
responses.text: Neither name belongs to anyone in this party, so the question does not quite land. If you mean Clauven and Deepwilla, I would say intelligence and wisdom are different tools, and Deepwilla builds things with hers while mine sits in a chair and reads.

responses.name: gemaux
responses.text: A mind's true measure is not in what it holds, but in the harmony it creates, whether through intricate gears or profound understanding. Both Deepw

responses.name: geptima
responses.text: Deepwilla’s creations and Clauven’s insights both serve us in different ways. Intelligence isn’t measured by competition; it’s by what you do with it to help others.

text: Take a moment to observe and comment on what Moonie is currently doing.

responses.name: deepwilla
responses.text: Those crystals are definitely resonating at a harmonic frequency.I wonder if the door mechanism is pressure-sensitive or if it's waiting for a specific tonal input.

responses.name: gemaux
responses.text: A door that seeks a harmonic frequency, or a tonal input... that sounds like a ballad waiting to be sung, or a secret whisper meant only for its stony ear.

responses.name: grokthar
responses.text: Looks like Moonie's waiting for the stream to kick off, just staring at that door with the glowing crystals. Nature teaches patience, prey doesn’t move until the trap is set. Let’s see if he figures the puzzle before something figures him.
